plan: Trim SPY to 20% to reduce concentration and meet mandate.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETF exposure.
Increase QQQI to 25% and QQQ to 20% for stable broad index and income.
Add DDOG at 5% as a high-quality tech name.
This plan prioritizes capital preservation and reduces overall portfolio risk.
Maintain a cash buffer of approximately 15-20%.
conservative.read: This conservative plan aims for a modest ~1.5%/month outlook, focusing on capital preservation. It significantly reduces leverage and concentration, but still carries market risk. Drawdowns should be lower, but growth potential is limited.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet concentration mandate.
Sell SOXL due to its flagged status and reduce overall leveraged ETF exposure.
Keep SPAL at 2% for minimal leveraged exposure.
Increase CRWD to 15% as a strong performing tech holding.
Buy PANW at 5% as a high-scoring new tech setup.
This plan balances growth with risk management, targeting a ~10% cash buffer.
balanced.read: This balanced plan targets ~2.5%/month with a focus on risk-first growth. It reduces overall leverage and concentration while adding quality tech. Expect moderate market volatility and drawdowns.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet concentration mandate.
Sell SOXL due to its flagged status, reallocating capital to stronger setups.
Increase SMH to 18% and CRWD to 20% to lean into strong sectors.
Buy DDOG at 7% and PANW at 5% as high-scoring new tech setups.
This plan uses concentration and active management for aggressive growth.
Maintain a small cash buffer of approximately 5%.
aggressive.read: This aggressive plan targets ~4%/month by leaning into high-scoring tech and sector concentration. It carries significantly higher risk, including larger potential drawdowns and the impact of leveraged ETF decay if used. Strict stop discipline is crucial to manage amplified losses.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet concentration mandate.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETF exposure.
Significantly increase QQQI to 40% for enhanced monthly distributions.
Reduce QQQ to 10% and CRWD to 5% to lower growth-oriented exposure.
This plan prioritizes steady monthly distributions and capital preservation.
Maintain a cash buffer of approximately 15%.
income.read: This income plan targets ~1%/month by rotating into income-focused ETFs. It prioritizes low drawdown and reliable income over growth, but distributions are variable and NAV can still fall in a selloff. This is not a savings account; market risk remains.
